mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5% and remains there for at least one month, the zero lower bound constraint becomes binding and monetary policy loses transmission effectiveness. Equity risk premiums widen as investors recognize policy exhaustion, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS=0.09% indicates near-zero bound regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in ZLB regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession risk. This triggers equity sell-offs as earnings expectations decline, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE monthly changes and 5-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% annualized rate while housing starts (HOUST) simultaneously fall below 500k units, indicating synchronized demand weakness across consumer and residential sectors. This dual contraction predicts negative equity returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth < 2% annualized AND HOUST < 500k units
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of dual-trigger instances, or the joint signal produces lower Rank IC than either component alone over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises, indicating supply-side weakness coupled with labor shedding. This combination signals stagflationary pressure and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines MoM AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of dual-trigger instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power beyond individual components (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows at a sustained rate above 200k per month over a 3-month rolling window, signaling robust labor market expansion. This persistent strength supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window in expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3% annualized rate while the federal funds rate remains below 1%, creating negative real rates. This real rate compression incentivizes equity allocation as a hedge against currency debasement, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: negative real rate regime with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of dual-trigger instances, or the negative real rate signal shows no correlation with equity inflows (correlation < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1% annualized rate in the most recent quarter, signaling late-cycle slowdown. This deceleration triggers downward earnings revisions and equity multiple compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 1%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) grows at a sustained rate above 2% annualized over a 3-month rolling window while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2.5% annualized, indicating healthy supply-side expansion without overheating. This benign supply regime supports margin expansion and positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth > 2% annualized AND CPIAUCSL 12-month growth < 2.5%
regime_hint: goldilocks supply expansion with price stability
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of dual-trigger instances, or the benign supply signal shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
